Flu.action: Rest, hydrate, and consult a doctor if symptoms worsen.
medicines: Paracetamol or acetaminophen for fever and aches
Ibuprofen for inflammation if tolerated
Cough suppressant or throat lozenges
precautions: Rest and limit physical activity
Stay hydrated with water and warm fluids
Avoid sharing utensils and cover your mouth when coughing
home_remedies: Warm broth to soothe the throat

Common Cold.action: Rest and stay hydrated. Symptoms usually resolve in 7-10 days.
medicines: Paracetamol for fever and headache
Decongestant spray or tablets for nasal congestion
Throat lozenges for soreness
precautions: Drink plenty of fluids
Rest and avoid cold exposure
Wash hands frequently to prevent spread

COVID-19.action: Isolate immediately and seek medical attention. Get tested.
medicines: Paracetamol for fever and discomfort
Prescription antivirals if advised by a doctor
Oxygen support if breathing becomes difficult
precautions: Isolate from others
Wear a mask and practice respiratory hygiene
Monitor oxygen saturation and temperature frequently
home_remedies: Use humidified air to ease cough
Gargle warm salt water for throat comfort

Dengue.action: Seek immediate medical attention. Monitor platelet count.
medicines: Paracetamol for fever and body pain
Oral rehydration salts to replace fluids
Avoid aspirin and ibuprofen unless directed by a doctor
precautions: Rest and drink plenty of fluids
Monitor platelet count and bleeding signs
Avoid mosquito exposure and use repellents
home_remedies: Coconut water or electrolyte drinks to stay hydrated

Malaria.description: Parasitic disease spread by infected mosquitoes
Malaria.action: Seek immediate medical attention. Antimalarial drugs required.
medicines: Prescription antimalarial medication as directed by a physician
Paracetamol for fever control
Fluids to prevent dehydration
precautions: Seek immediate medical attention
Sleep under a treated mosquito net
Avoid self-medicating without confirmation
home_remedies: Drink plenty of fluids and nutrient-rich broths

Typhoid.description: Bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi
Typhoid.action: Antibiotics required. Consult a doctor immediately.
medicines: Prescription antibiotics as directed by a doctor
Oral rehydration salts for dehydration
Paracetamol for fever management
precautions: Avoid contaminated food and water
Rest and follow a bland diet
Seek medical evaluation promptly
home_remedies: Eat simple, light foods such as khichdi or broths

Pneumonia.description: Lung infection causing inflammation of air sacs
Pneumonia.action: Immediate medical attention required. May need hospitalization.
medicines: Antibiotics or antiviral therapy as prescribed
Cough expectorant or inhaler if prescribed
Fever reducers such as paracetamol
precautions: Keep the chest warm and rest
Avoid smoke and irritants
Seek medical care if breathing becomes difficult
home_remedies: Use a humidifier to ease breathing

Tuberculosis.description: Bacterial infection primarily affecting the lungs
Tuberculosis.action: Consult a pulmonologist. Long-term antibiotic treatment needed.
medicines: Prescription anti-tuberculosis medication under supervision
Vitamins and nutritional support as recommended
precautions: Follow the full treatment course exactly
Avoid spreading germs by covering mouth while coughing
Attend follow-up exams regularly
home_remedies: Rest well and maintain balanced nutrition
Avoid crowded indoor spaces during active infection

Diabetes.description: Metabolic disease with high blood sugar levels
Diabetes.action: Consult an endocrinologist. Lifestyle changes and medication needed.
medicines: Metformin or insulin therapy as prescribed
Oral glucose-lowering medication if advised
precautions: Monitor blood sugar regularly
Follow a balanced diet and exercise plan
Avoid high-sugar foods and processed snacks
home_remedies: Eat fiber-rich foods like oats and leafy greens

Hypertension.action: Monitor BP regularly. Medication and lifestyle changes required.
medicines: ACE inhibitors or beta-blockers as prescribed
Diuretics or calcium channel blockers if advised
precautions: Reduce salt intake
Limit alcohol and caffeine
Practice stress management and exercise regularly
home_remedies: Drink hibiscus tea or warm lemon water

Asthma.description: Chronic respiratory condition with airway inflammation
Asthma.action: Use prescribed inhalers. Avoid triggers and consult a pulmonologist.
medicines: Inhaled bronchodilator rescue inhaler
Inhaled corticosteroid maintenance therapy as prescribed
precautions: Avoid known triggers like smoke and dust
Keep inhalers accessible
Monitor breathing and peak flow if advised

Jaundice.description: Yellowing of skin due to high bilirubin levels
Jaundice.action: Consult a gastroenterologist. Liver function tests needed.
medicines: Prescription liver support medication if advised
Avoid unnecessary pain relievers that affect the liver
precautions: Avoid alcohol and fatty foods
Stay hydrated and eat a healthy diet
Consult a doctor for liver function tests
home_remedies: Drink plenty of water and fresh fruit juices
Eat light, easy-to-digest foods such as soups

Chickenpox.description: Highly contagious viral disease causing blister-like rash
Chickenpox.action: Rest and avoid scratching. Antiviral medication may help.
medicines: Calamine lotion to soothe itching
Antihistamines for itching relief
Paracetamol for fever if needed
precautions: Avoid scratching blisters
Keep the skin clean and dry
Isolate to prevent spread

Measles.description: Highly contagious viral disease preventable by vaccine
Measles.action: Isolate and seek medical care. Supportive treatment required.
medicines: Vitamin A supplement if prescribed
Paracetamol for fever and discomfort
precautions: Isolate from others
Stay hydrated and rest
Consult healthcare provider for supportive treatment
home_remedies: Use cool compresses for eye discomfort

Anemia.description: Condition with insufficient healthy red blood cells
Anemia.action: Iron supplements and dietary changes. Consult a hematologist.
medicines: Iron supplements and folic acid as prescribed
Vitamin B12 or vitamin C supplements if needed
precautions: Eat iron-rich foods like spinach and lean meats
Avoid tea or coffee with meals
Schedule follow-up blood tests
home_remedies: Consume citrus fruits with iron-rich meals

Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of stomach and intestines (stomach flu)
Gastroenteritis.action: Stay hydrated. Seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
medicines: Oral rehydration salts
Anti-nausea medication if prescribed
Antidiarrheal agents when recommended
precautions: Avoid dairy and spicy foods
Stay hydrated with electrolyte solutions
Eat bland foods until symptoms improve
home_remedies: Sip ginger tea or mint water
Eat bananas, rice, applesauce, and toast (BRAT diet)

Urinary Tract Infection.description: Bacterial infection in the urinary system
Urinary Tract Infection.action: Antibiotics required. Drink plenty of water.
medicines: Antibiotics prescribed by a doctor
Pain reliever such as paracetamol for discomfort
precautions: Drink plenty of water
Urinate after intercourse
Avoid irritants like strong soaps and fragrances
home_remedies: Warm compress on the lower abdomen

Migraine.action: Rest in a dark quiet room. Take prescribed migraine medication.
medicines: Ibuprofen or acetaminophen for pain
Triptans or prescription migraine medication if advised
precautions: Rest in a quiet, dark room
Avoid bright lights and strong smells
Stay hydrated and maintain regular meals
home_remedies: Apply a cold pack to the forehead

Arthritis.description: Inflammation of one or more joints
Arthritis.action: Physiotherapy and anti-inflammatory medications. Consult a rheumatologist.
medicines: NSAIDs such as ibuprofen for pain relief
Topical pain relievers or prescription anti-inflammatory drugs
precautions: Keep joints warm and supported
Avoid repetitive stress on affected joints
Follow a physical therapy routine

Anxiety Disorder.description: Mental health disorder with persistent worry
Anxiety Disorder.action: Seek professional mental health support. Therapy and medication may help.
medicines: Prescription anti-anxiety medication if advised
Low-dose antidepressants for chronic symptoms
precautions: Practice stress reduction techniques
Avoid caffeine and stimulants
Maintain a regular sleep schedule

Hypothyroidism.description: Underactive thyroid gland producing insufficient hormones
Hypothyroidism.action: Thyroid hormone replacement therapy. Regular TSH monitoring.
medicines: Levothyroxine thyroid hormone replacement
Take medication on an empty stomach as directed
precautions: Monitor thyroid levels regularly
Avoid soy and high-fiber meals around dosing time
Follow your doctor’s dosage instructions
home_remedies: Eat selenium-rich foods like nuts and seeds

Kidney Stones.description: Hard mineral deposits forming inside the kidneys
Kidney Stones.action: Increase water intake. Pain management and possible medical procedures.
medicines: Pain relievers such as ibuprofen or paracetamol
Alpha blockers if prescribed to ease stone passage
precautions: Drink plenty of water
Avoid high-oxalate foods if advised
Seek care if pain is severe or urine is bloody

GERD.description: Gastroesophageal reflux disease causing acid reflux
GERD.action: Antacids and lifestyle changes. Consult a gastroenterologist.
medicines: Antacids for immediate relief
H2 blockers or proton pump inhibitors if prescribed
precautions: Avoid spicy or fatty foods
Eat smaller meals and avoid lying down after eating
Elevate the head while sleeping
home_remedies: Chew gum after meals to increase saliva production

Depression.description: Mental health disorder affecting mood and daily functioning
Depression.action: Seek professional mental health support. Therapy and medication available.
medicines: Prescription antidepressants if recommended
Supplements such as vitamin D if advised
precautions: Seek regular mental health support
Keep a consistent sleep and activity schedule
Avoid alcohol and drugs as coping mechanisms

Appendicitis.description: Inflammation of the appendix requiring urgent treatment
Appendicitis.action: EMERGENCY: Seek immediate medical care. Surgery may be required.
medicines: Do not self-medicate; emergency surgical care is required
precautions: Seek immediate medical attention
Avoid eating until evaluated by a doctor
Do not take strong painkillers that mask symptoms without guidance
home_remedies: No home remedies; this is a medical emergency.
